diagnosis: The player spawns at (176, 816) inside a fully enclosed 32×32 solid box formed by the bottom two rows of the grid (rows 22-23) and solid tiles at columns 5-7 in row 21. The trace shows the player stationary for ~6.3 s, then moving right only 294 px over the next 12 s while y stays fixed at 818 px — confirming they never left the spawn cell. Zero deaths, zero falls, and a 1/5 rating with the explicit comment 'spawn inside a box… cannot get out' make this a hard geometry bug, not a difficulty or pacing issue.

lessons.lesson: Always validate that the spawn tile has at least one orthogonally adjacent empty tile before finalizing a level; a single solid-tile enclosure turns the entire playtest into a hard lock.
conditions: spawn marker placed without runtime connectivity check
lessons.outcome: player is trapped immediately, cannot move, rates experience 1/5 and quits
